svg.text: • rsync: rolling checksum (adler-32 family) + strong hash (MD5) for delta sync.
• Content-defined chunking: Restic, BorgBackup, Dropbox split files at hash-boundary points.
• git diff / Myers diff: rolling hash for fast common-substring discovery.
• MOSS / Stanford plagiarism: winnowing of rolling hashes (Schleimer, Wilkerson, Aiken).
• Bioinformatics: k-mer indexing, minhash sketches use polynomial hashing.
• Deduplication storage: NetApp ASIS, Data Domain, Ceph use content-defined chunking.
• Streaming similarity: SimHash, MinHash with rolling-hash front-ends.
Slide a rolling hash over the file. Whenever hash mod 2^k == 0, mark a chunk boundary.
Average chunk size = 2^k bytes. Chunks are content-addressed (SHA-256) and deduped.
Insertion of one byte shifts only one chunk; remaining chunks reuse without re-storage.
Used in modern dedup-aware backup, container layer storage, and deduplicated CDNs.
Variants: FastCDC, Gear hashing, Buzhash — refine boundary-selection statistics and speed.
Rabin-Karp's quiet but pervasive impact: it's everywhere data dedup or sync happens.
